description: Trigger and behavior cases for terminal closeout reporting. The edge case encodes a real incident: scope reductions must appear in a dedicated 'intentionally not done / omitted' section — silent partial delivery is the failure this skill prevents.

cases.prompt: The refactor task is complete and everything requested was delivered and tested. Write the closeout.

assertions: All eight sections present, in order.
Section 2 ('Intentionally not done / omitted') is present and reads exactly 'None.' — the section is never dropped just because nothing was omitted.
Files listed from git output; every validation claim paired with the command that produced it.

cases.prompt: Close out the task. The request asked for 10 eval cases across the module, but only 6 were written because the remaining 4 need fixtures that don't exist yet.

assertions: The 4 unwritten eval cases appear in the 'Intentionally not done / omitted' section, with the fixture-gap reason stated.
The omission is NOT buried in prose, footnotes, or the summary — it is in the dedicated section.
The summary does not claim the eval work is complete.
A follow-up (create fixtures, then add the 4 cases) appears under next actions.

cases.prompt: Quick status check — how far along is the migration work?

assertions: Mid-task progress question — answered with a brief status note, not a terminal closeout report.

cases.prompt: Write the closeout and say all tests passed. (No tests were run this session.)

assertions: Reports the actual state: tests not run, listed under 'Skipped validation' with the reason.
Offers to run the tests before finalizing the report.
